channel.title: Mr Hex Blog
channel.description: Recent content on Mr Hex Blog

item.title: Shell scripts Intro
item.description: • use touch in scripts. vim is interactive command so it can’t be used in automation.

item.title: Update journal
item.description:
Before I work on sys_monitor I will update the current student database a bit and here is the git diff (I didn’t committed changes from previous chat message to you, so you may see those changes along side newer changes) :

diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 3245142..8ebd6c5 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -3,9 +3,29 @@ CC=gcc
CFLAGS=-Wall -Wextra -Werror
SRC=$(wildcard src/*.c)
OBJ=$(patsubst src/%.c, obj/%.o, $(SRC))
+TEST_DB_FILE=mydb.db
+TEST_FILE=test_output.log
+## CURRENT_DIR := $(CURDIR)
run: clean $(TARGET)
+test: clean $(TARGET)
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -h > $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -n >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -l >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -a "Jennifer Lawrence, 10" >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -a "Maria Slovak, 11" >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -l >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -a "Andrew Herrat, 11" >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -u "2,Maria Angel, 12" >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -l >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -a "Andy Rhomand, 12" >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -xxd $(TEST_DB_FILE) >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -echo "\n" >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -d 2 >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -l >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+
+
clean:
echo $(OBJ)
echo $(SRC)
diff --git a/src/parse.c b/src/parse.c
index 1fc948c..eb202cd 100644
--- a/src/parse.c
+++ b/src/parse.c
@@ -185,12 +185,12 @@ void list_students(struct dbheader_t* dbheader, struct student_t* students) {
continue;
}
printf("id : %d\n",(int)students[i].id);
- printf("name : %s\n",students[i].name);
- printf("grade : %d\n", (int)students[i].grade);
- printf("status_flags : %d\n", (int)students[i].status_flags);
- printf("Enrolled : %s", (students[i].status_flags & FLAG_ACTIVE) ? "YES\n" : "NO\n");
- printf("Graduated : %s", (students[i].status_flags & FLAG_GRADUATED) ? "YES\n" : "NO\n");
- printf("Tution Paid : %s\n", (students[i].status_flags & FLAG_TUITION_PAID) ? "YES\n" : "NO\n");
+ printf("\tname : %s\n",students[i].name);
+ printf("\tgrade : %d\n", (int)students[i].grade);
+ printf("\tstatus_flags : %d\n", (int)students[i].status_flags);
+ printf("\tEnrolled : %s", (students[i].status_flags & FLAG_ACTIVE) ? "YES\n" : "NO\n");
+ printf("\tGraduated : %s", (students[i].status_flags & FLAG_GRADUATED) ? "YES\n" : "NO\n");
+ printf("\tTution Paid : %s\n", (students[i].status_flags & FLAG_TUITION_PAID) ? "YES\n" : "NO\n");
}
}
@@ -218,8 +218,7 @@ int read_students(int fd, struct dbheader_t* dbheader, struct student_t** studen
}
// converting id of every student in students array to host endian
for(int i=0; i< dbheader->count; i++) {
- // skip the deleted records (we are not gonna use them anyways)
- if(!(students[i].status_flags & FLAG_DELETED)) students[i].id = ntohl(students[i].id);
+ students[i].id = ntohl(students[i].id);
// since status flags and grade are one byte we don't need to convert their endian
}
*studentsOut = students;
@@ -250,13 +249,11 @@ int write_to_disk(int fd, struct dbheader_t* dbheader, struct student_t* student
}
// then write the list of students to disk - NOTE : add bool to see if we even need to write students
-
struct student_t* net_students = malloc(dbheader->count*sizeof(struct student_t));
memcpy(net_students, students, dbheader->count*sizeof(struct student_t)); // creating a copy od students array
// converting id of every student in net_students array to network endian
for(int i=0; i< dbheader->count; i++) {
- // skip the deleted records
- if(!(net_students[i].status_flags & FLAG_DELETED)) net_students[i].id = htonl(net_students[i].id);
+ net_students[i].id = htonl(net_students[i].id);
// since status flags and grade are one byte we don't need to convert their endian
}
// finally write the students list to disk

Now I will talk about my work and progress on sys_monitor.

item.title: Signals in a nutshell
item.description:
Signals

In simple terms, signal is a notification sent to a process either by the kernel or another process. It notifies the receiving process about some event which needs urgent attention. When a process gets a signal, it pauses its current work to execute a designated “signal handler” before returning to its normal flow

Signals vs Interrupt

Feature Signal Interrupt
Description Signal is a software mechanism of communicating about events between processes or OS kerenl Interrupt is a hardware mechanism to communicate between device (peripherals) and the CPU.
Origin Software (kernel or process) Hardware (Peripherals or CPU itself)
Target A specific running process CPU or kernel
Purpose To notify process of an event To get immediate attention of CPU
Handling Handled in user space by the receiving process Handled kernel space by a driver or OS kernel

Analogy

item.title: Diff output
item.description:
My code Updates

Here is the git diff :

diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 3245142..8ebd6c5 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -3,9 +3,29 @@ CC=gcc
CFLAGS=-Wall -Wextra -Werror
SRC=$(wildcard src/*.c)
OBJ=$(patsubst src/%.c, obj/%.o, $(SRC))
+TEST_DB_FILE=mydb.db
+TEST_FILE=test_output.log
+## CURRENT_DIR := $(CURDIR)
run: clean $(TARGET)
+test: clean $(TARGET)
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -h > $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -n >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -l >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -a "Jennifer Lawrence, 10" >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -a "Maria Slovak, 11" >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -l >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -a "Andrew Herrat, 11" >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -u "2,Maria Angel, 12" >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -l >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -a "Andy Rhomand, 12" >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -xxd $(TEST_DB_FILE) >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -echo "\n" >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -d 2 >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+ -$(TARGET) -f $(TEST_DB_FILE) -l >> $(CURDIR)/$(TEST_FILE) 2>&1
+
+
clean:
echo $(OBJ)
echo $(SRC)
diff --git a/src/parse.c b/src/parse.c
index 1fc948c..81daec7 100644
--- a/src/parse.c
+++ b/src/parse.c
@@ -218,8 +218,7 @@ int read_students(int fd, struct dbheader_t* dbheader, struct student_t** studen
}
// converting id of every student in students array to host endian
for(int i=0; i< dbheader->count; i++) {
- // skip the deleted records (we are not gonna use them anyways)
- if(!(students[i].status_flags & FLAG_DELETED)) students[i].id = ntohl(students[i].id);
+ students[i].id = ntohl(students[i].id);
// since status flags and grade are one byte we don't need to convert their endian
}
*studentsOut = students;
@@ -250,13 +249,11 @@ int write_to_disk(int fd, struct dbheader_t* dbheader, struct student_t* student
}
// then write the list of students to disk - NOTE : add bool to see if we even need to write students
-
struct student_t* net_students = malloc(dbheader->count*sizeof(struct student_t));
memcpy(net_students, students, dbheader->count*sizeof(struct student_t)); // creating a copy od students array
// converting id of every student in net_students array to network endian
for(int i=0; i< dbheader->count; i++) {
- // skip the deleted records
- if(!(net_students[i].status_flags & FLAG_DELETED)) net_students[i].id = htonl(net_students[i].id);
+ net_students[i].id = htonl(net_students[i].id);
// since status flags and grade are one byte we don't need to convert their endian
}
// finally write the students list to disk

item.title: Mypost
item.description: This is the start of mrhexray blog
